data.title: RH reported Q2 earnings of $2.93/share, missing estimate of $3.20/share. Revenue $899.15M, below $904.64M estimate. CEO cites industry-leading growth.
data.description: RH (NYSE: RH) announced their second-quarter results, which fell below expectations. The company reported earnings of $2.93 per share, missing the estimated $3.20 per share. Their quarterly revenue was $899.15 million, below the expected $904.64 million. Despite challenges such as tariff uncertainty and a tough housing market, RH still achieved significant growth with an 8.4% increase in revenue and a 13.7% rise in demand. CEO Gary Friedman acknowledged these obstacles but highlighted the company's strong performance. RH also adjusted its fiscal 2025 revenue forecast. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Adobe beats Q3 expectations with $5.31 EPS and $5.99B revenue; raises fiscal 2025 outlook. CEO states AI creative applications leading. Adjusted EPS guidance raised to $20.80-$20.85 for 2025.
data.description: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) exceeded expectations in the third quarter and revised its fiscal 2025 outlook. The company reported earnings of $5.31 per share, surpassing the expected $5.18 per share. Quarterly revenue stood at $5.99 billion, beating the estimated $5.91 billion. Adobe's AI creative applications segment demonstrated significant growth, with AI-influenced Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) surpassing $5 billion and AI-first ARR exceeding the year-end target of $250 million. Shantanu Narayen, Adobe's chair and CEO, highlighted the company's leadership in the AI creative applications category. The revised fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS guidance is now between $20.80 and $20.85.

data.title: Archer aims to join White House eVTOL pilot program with U.S. airlines & cities like United Airlines. Includes DOT & FAA. #Archer #ACHR
data.description: In a recent announcement, Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) shared its intention to join the White House's eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP). This program, established alongside U.S. airlines such as United Airlines and various interested cities, aims to speed up electric air taxi operations in the United States. The initiative was first introduced in June 2025 as a part of the "Unleashing Drone Dominance" Executive Order. The Department of Transportation (DOT) and Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) are facilitating this federal program.

data.title: JackRabbit partners with DICK'S Sporting Goods to expand its retail presence, offering compact EVs nationwide.
data.description: JackRabbit, a popular brand of micro ebikes and small electric vehicles, is expanding its range of compact, all-terrain transportation options for outdoor enthusiasts across the country. The company, based in San Diego, made the announcement on September 12, 2025. JackRabbit is known for its innovative approach to micromobility, offering ultra-light and portable small EVs and micro ebikes.

data.title: Asti Week debuts in NYC Sep 15-19, 2025 with Asti Spumante & Moscato d'Asti across 15 restaurants, celebrating Italian wines.
data.description: Between September 15 and 19, 2025, New York City will host the inaugural Asti Week, a unique event showcasing the sophistication and adaptability of Asti Spumante and Moscato d'Asti, renowned sparkling wines from Piedmont, Italy. Aiming to celebrate these iconic beverages, the event will feature various activities and tastings to engage wine enthusiasts throughout the city.

data.title: Kay Properties launches "1031 DST Digest" magazine providing guidance to accredited investors on DST investments and 1031 exchanges.
data.description: Kay Properties & Investments, based in Torrance, California, has introduced the "1031 DST Digest," a new investor resource aimed at accredited investors interested in Delaware Statutory Trust (DST) investments and 1031 exchange strategies. This magazine offers practical guidance and detailed strategies to individuals looking into DST and 1031 exchange opportunities. The 1031 DST Digest aligns with Kay Properties' commitment to providing top-tier investor education. This initiative serves as a comprehensive tool for investors, highlighting important information and insights within the DST investment realm.

data.title: Lendbuzz files for IPO with SEC, utilizing AI and ML for consumer credit risk, aiming to list common stock on The NASDAQ.
data.description: Boston-based financial technology company Lendbuzz Inc. has revealed its plans for an initial public offering (IPO) of its common stock. The company, which leverages artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms to enhance the evaluation of consumer credit risk and increase credit availability for vehicle purchases, filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Lendbuzz aims to list its common stock on The NASDAQ exchange. This move signifies a significant step for Lendbuzz in reaching a broader investor base and expanding its market presence.

data.title: Inflation rising to 2.9% YoY, OPEN stock rallied 80% overnight. Fed likely to cut rates next week, uncertainty on the amount.
data.description: Inflation remains a concern for the Federal Reserve as it is not decreasing rapidly enough, with housing potentially showing signs of improvement from its recent decline. The most recent CPI report for August revealed that inflation rose to 2.9% year-over-year from 2.7% in July, while core inflation held steady at 3.1%. Monthly inflation also increased by approximately 0.4% on a seasonally adjusted basis. Despite these numbers, the Fed is expected to cut rates next week, with a 100% probability of a rate reduction, although the likelihood of a 50 basis points cut versus a 25 basis points cut stands at 7.5%. The market anticipates a rate cut in September, with the key questions now being the extent of the cut and the Fed's confidence in future actions. Certain sectors such as food and shelter are experiencing persistent inflation, which may deter the Fed from implementing aggressive rate cuts. However, the overall trend suggests that a easing monetary policy is gaining momentum. The upcoming FOMC meeting will provide further insights. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds in demand as crypto market hits $4T. Ether ETFs gain $230M after $800M outflows last week.
data.description: As of Thursday, Ether ETFs saw a positive shift with net inflows exceeding $230 million, rebounding from the previous week's net outflows of approximately $800 million.

data.title: Cocrystal Pharma, Inc. announces $13 million offering; $4.7 million upfront with potential $8.3 million from warrants.
data.description: An initial payment of $4.7 million was made, with the potential for an additional approximate total of $8.3 million in gross proceeds upon the full exercise of warrants.

data.title: Prenetics reports remarkable growth in second quarter 2025, with $32.3M revenue, aiming for $100M annual revenue by year-end. IM8 achieved $5.9M revenue in Aug 2025, pursuing $9M monthly goal. Company holding 228 Bitcoin, adding 1 daily. Target break-even in Q1 2026.	Read more: https://www.benzinga.com/pressreleases/25/09/g23399835/prenetics-announces-second-quarter-2025-financial-results-with-im8-on-track-to-achieve-100m-annual-reven
data.description: Prenetics Global Limited, a health sciences company, reported impressive financial results for the second quarter of 2025 with revenue of $17.7 million and a gross profit of $7.3 million. They also increased their revenue guidance range for FY2025 to $85-$100 million. IM8, their supplement brand, achieved $5.9 million in revenue in August 2025, marking a remarkable CAGR of over 3,100%. Prenetics added a total of 228 Bitcoins to their Treasury, accumulating 1 BTC daily. With a cash balance of $90 million and a clear path to profitability in Q1 2026, Prenetics aims to achieve break-even and be EBITDA positive. IM8 Health demonstrated strong growth, with monthly revenue surging from $581,000 to $5.9 million in August 2025. The brand has processed over 300,000 customer orders, delivering over 8 million servings of its products. Prenetics also divested ACT Genomics and plans to increase their Bitcoin accumulation rate, believing in Bitcoin as a store of value. Their full-year revenue guidance for 2025 is between $85-$100 million, and they expect to achieve break-even in Q1 2026. Prenetics aims to deliver significant long-term value to shareholders through their strategic vision and operational performance.
